programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: In a particularly active episode, Armando Iannucci performs a daring stunt, Kumail Nanjiani moves bowling pins with oven gloves, and Joanna Page unwisely attempts a handstand.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A hair stylist meets a nutritionist with a bit of swag. A conspiracy theorist is matched with a science teacher who also wrestles. And an opera singer serenades his date.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: Up a long garden path in Leeds is a Victorian villa thats Jawad and Charlies labour of love. And in Brixton, George hopes for a clean sweep with a redundant chimney.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: In Herriot Country, Matt takes to the skies, Mike meets the ancestors of the famous Mouseman furniture maker and Janice visits a stunning lavender farm.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: We return to the Algarve with Cheryl and Graham, who swapped life in Surrey to run a riverside BandB. And Brian and Paula are on the move after closing their popular eatery in France.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: A new series with our favourite family-run estate agents. In Almería, the Garners juggle wow-factor villas and a wildcard. Lisa and John face tough compromises on the Costa Blanca.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Susie and John met on a dancing holiday in Spain and are now looking for the perfect holiday home around Estepona. Jean Johansson has lined up five properties for their £100,000 budget.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is house hunting with John and Debbie around the Mar Menor on the Costa Calida in south-east Spain. The couple want a two-bedroom holiday home thats near the beach. They have a budget of £80,000 to buy an apartment or a townhouse.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Sasha transform their courtyard before back-to-back weddings commence. Anna and Edward spruce up their roof for a wine weekend. Roger and Louisa create a new ensuite.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil look back at Alex and Danny Moores race for space, as their lofty ambition of converting the attic in their Preston semi took shape.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: From Perth the first to host is 38-year old first aid manager Jacqui. Jacqui plans to impress her guests with hearty cuisine and is serving up a selection of pates, roast venison and a homely sticky toffee pud.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the second day of the competition in and around Perth and the turn of aspiring actor Daniel to host. Daniel is hoping to impress with his twist on a traditional Scottish menu, serving Spaghetti Hagginaise for his main and home-made Irn Bru ice cream for dessert.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third instalment of the weeks brand competition comes from Basque-born Lorea and shes on a mission to showcase the culinary delights of her native home in Northern Spain. Loreas fiesta commences with a pink cocktail and an exotic quartet flown over from the Basque country especially to impress.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the fourth night of the competition in and around Perth and the turn of feisty Jenna to try and impress. She is aiming for a night of perfection, but last nights host Lorea is baffled by her decision to put plastic on her dining room floor.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final day of this competition in and around Perth and its the turn of Kiwi-born Craig to host. Craig is hoping that his land and sea menu can blow the competition out of the water. Hes gone for locally caught prime lobster and has had wild boar brought in especially from the Scottish highlands.

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: Edinburgh locals perform the one song that tells their story - from the tune a 22-year-old played at his mums funeral to the Zach Bryan track that helped a former soldiers PTSD.

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: In Switzerland, Paul travels up front on the stunning Glacier Express and drives a funicular train to the Reichenbach Falls, where Sherlock Holmes met his fictional demise.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: 12-year-old Ruby-Rose is ready to move up from dog sitting to a dog of her own. Could Georgie the cheeky Yorkie be the one? And will ex-cabbie Danny give Bella the collie a lift?

programme.title: Sky Coppers
programme.desc: The team join the search for a woman who has left a suicide note and vanished into the night. And officers are shocked when they arrive at a house to discover a gang cruelly breeding dangerous dogs in horrific conditions.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britains best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the weeks biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.title: Miss Peregrines Home for Peculiar Children
programme.desc: When teenager Jacob travels to a mystical island he discovers some crumbling ruins. As he explores the rooms and hallways he learns that its previous residents possessed incredible powers, and that they had some very scary enemies.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Critically ill dialysis patient Gillian desperately needs fluids, meaning the crew of East Anglian Air Ambulance needs to make a tough decision.

programme.desc: Critically ill dialysis patient Gillian desperately needs fluids, meaning the crew of East Anglian Air Ambulance needs to make a tough decision.

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Japans Shoplifting Pensioners: Japanese prisons have become retirement homes for a rising number of elderly convicts. Krishnan Guru-Murthy investigates the countrys geriatric crime wave.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.desc: With three million dashcams now in vehicles, a look at some of Britains worst driving offenders.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Experts predict one of the shortest and warmest winters on record; as a result, companies risk financial ruin and communities face deprivation unless the drivers can deliver vital supplies before their window of opportunity slams shut.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: While investigating the connection between the abduction of several teenagers and a rural religious cult, Mulder and Scully discover the secret tests being performed on the children using the `Purity Control alien DNA from `The Erlenmeyer Flask, which leads Mulder to the Crew-Cut Man, Deep Throats murderer.

programme.desc: Conor Woodman checks out the scams in Sin City. From swanky casino doormen to those who live in the citys storm drains, he finds countless tricksters waiting to rip him off.

programme.desc: While Carol and Susan are away, Ross and the friends babysit his newborn son Ben, but Ross is disgusted when Phoebe tests the temperature of some bottled breast milk on her wrist. Meanwhile, Joey competes with a fellow cologne salesman.

programme.desc: Meeting Jolene who has been doing tarot card reading professionally for over twenty-four years, she is extremely passionate about the positive impact it is able to have on people.

programme.desc: On the lookout for a high-value car thats been taken from a dealership during a test drive. A builders van is taken from a house driveway while its owner pops briefly inside to collect his tools and equipment.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The traffic cops are alerted when a car is spotted by police cameras being driven with no insurance. PC Andy Swift stops a car after suspecting its not insured. The driver gets straight out and is very aggressive.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Even though its only just after 10am, a woman is suspected of drink-driving and traffic cop Matt Copestake is called to investigate. Traffic cop Andy Swift is on the trail of a driver who has allegedly failed to stop after a collision.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: West Yorkshire is one of the UKs highest crime areas. With this proud region seeing its criminal activity levels spiralling, the Interceptors use every tool in their arsenal to deliver justice.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Runaway drivers are convinced to stop by the threat of 50,000 volts. A drink driver resists arrest, earning a face full of incapacitating PAVA spray. A fresh-faced and uninsured driver tries to pass himself off as his 52-year-old dad.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Chris Spencer and the team chase a Golf GTI that is racing through the streets at three times the legal limit. Reports of gunshots at a house party on a residential street lead to a full-scale firearms operation.

programme.title: Shoplifters and Scammers: At War With The Law
programme.desc: Documentary charting methods used to thwart criminals targeting shops, beginning with a private crimes team employed to catch shoplifters in Londons West End.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A patient with a stomach ache suddenly goes into labour, sister Vicky has to perform CPR, and volunteer Jane is determined to solve a mystery involving a missing cutlery tray that disappeared from the dishwasher.

programme.title: Air Force One
programme.desc: High-altitude thriller in which the presidential aircraft, Air Force One, is hijacked by Russian communist radicals demanding the release of the Kazakhstani leader. With the First Lady and her family trapped on board, the hijackers threaten to kill one hostage every half-hour until their demands are met. What they dont know is that the president himself is hiding in the hold and formulating a plan to save his family.

programme.title: Rise of the Footsoldier 3
programme.desc: Ruthless Essex gangster Pat Tate peddles pills, snorts coke and crushes anyone who gets in his way of his quest for power. Yet, he ends up in prison after being double-crossed. Undeterred, he establishes himself inside, and prepares for his release, when he plans to reclaim his turf with violence and no mercy.

programme.desc: Learn how to say hello in over 10 different languages with the Hello Song, as well as singing along with classics including Im a little teapot, Baa Baa Black sheep and Itsy Bitsy Spider.

programme.title: Welcome to the Island
programme.desc: Mama bird builds a nest on Coco for her little chicks but Coco isnt happy with all the commotion. When the bird goes to get more twigs, one of the chicks almost falls out of the nest! Luckily Coco is there to save the day.

programme.title: Hungry Henry
programme.desc: Henry searches for his favourite dish and reveals how different foods are prepared.

programme.title: What a Wonderful Day
programme.desc: Its a lovely brand new day, time to wake up, time to play.

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes
programme.desc: When we meet somebody everywhere we go, we say hello. Ciao, Konnichi wa, Marhaba - Hello has different names - in Italian, Japanese or Arabic. Lets greet our friends from all around the world with a nice hello in their language.

programme.title: Teeny and Tinys Classroom
programme.desc: Teeny and Tiny are two happy and mischievous friends that share with us their riddles and games. As we follow the antics of the tiny friends, we discover shapes, colours and quantities.

programme.title: Giggle Wiggle
programme.desc: Moving and dancing and encouraging children to have fun by imitating the choreography they see on screen. A series of animals help keep children moving to the sound of catchy songs and rhymes.

programme.title: Snail Trail
programme.desc: Mr Snail meets a beautiful bee and discovers the secret of how bees make honey. The bee as puts the nectar she sucked out of a flower into a honeycomb and seals it up with wax, creating sweet and tasty honey.

programme.title: Happy Birthday to You
programme.desc: Make your child a star on BabyTV by creating their very own personalized birthday greeting. Using a digital photo, parents can create a clip for their child which can be viewed online or submitted to BabyTV for the chance of being featured on-air.

programme.title: BugnPlay to the Rescue
programme.desc: The three buggies play hide and seek. Will Hop be able to find Laz and Butterson? And will Butterson be able to get out of his hiding place? You dont want to miss out on the fun.

programme.title: Zeinas Detective Agency
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Confused Garden Hoe
programme.desc: A garden hoe arrives at Zeinas doorstep, forgetting what it is used for. Using her detective skill and her best friend Roy, Zeina discovers that a garden hoe is used for playing golf... is it?

programme.title: Billy Bam Bam Songs series
programme.desc: Think you can guess under which cup the candy is hidden? Come and see if you can help Bam Bam out. Its not so easy!

programme.title: Dino and the Egg Hunt
programme.sub-title: The Egg Hunters Climb the Giant Flower and Search the Swamp
programme.desc: Dino and his friend go in search of the two missing eggs. On their quest they climb a giant flower and search an ice cave.

programme.desc: A chance for everyone to learn all about shapes with Chopper Charlie.

programme.title: Mikes Bike

programme.title: Pix and Leo

programme.title: Crafty Rafty

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes
programme.desc: In this fun and popular counting rhyme for kids, the five little hungry mice are climbing a pot of rice to have a yummy feast.

programme.title: Mini Matinee

programme.title: Draco
programme.desc: Join Draco as he opens up a present and discovers many colourful boxes in it which he opens up one by one. By playing this game, he learns the difference between opposites open and close and finds suitable boxes to place all his toy cars in!

programme.title: The World Is A Wonder
programme.desc: In a tropical forest, rain waters a banana tree bulb which sends out strong roots! A little seedling sprouts and grows leaves and flowers on a banana tree! Join the birds and bees that enjoy sweet nectar! As purple flowers wilt, bunches of green bananas turn into tasty yellow ones that the monkeys eat!

programme.title: Baywatch

programme.title: Baywatch

programme.desc: Jill (Shawn Weatherly) loses her first drowning victim; an inept would-be lifeguard (Jeff Altman) blows his chances on the beach. Christopher Rich also guest stars. With David Hasselhoff.

programme.desc: The vast landscape of the Cairngorms attracts thousands each year. Coinneach Rankin sets out on a multi-day expedition to learn why its mountains are a paradise for climbers, runners and so many others.

programme.title: Leonardo da Vinci - Deisciobal an Eòlais
programme.desc: In Milan, Leonardo joins Duke Sforzas court, begins writing treatises, and paints a monumental fresco depicting the Last Supper.

programme.desc: Cuirm Òran Ùr aig Celtic Connections a comharrachadh òrain ùra Gàidhlig. Òran Ùr concert at Celtic Connections celebrating new Gaelic songs.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing wants to make a pretty picture for Sula but has a little accident with the glue pot.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: It seems that everyone has a knack for doing something thats special to them, everyone but poor Bing.

programme.title: Sionnach agus Maigheach
programme.desc: Brush decides to start a ladies club and invites all the ladies of the forest for brunch. When Fox and Tusk try to join in, dressed as ladies, Brush soon discovers their true identity and kicks them out. Offended, Fox and Tusk decide to start a club.

programme.title: S E Iasg a ThAnnam
programme.desc: A butterfly fish shows off his good looks, describes where he lives and explains why he is covered with bright colours. He also demonstrates how he keeps safe from predators at night.

programme.title: Cleasan Ataidh and Tilidh
programme.desc: Abney plants seeds in the moonlight. In the morning some have grown into lovely big cabbages. Abney and Teal decide to help out one that is struggling, which leads to big trouble.

programme.title: Peicein
programme.sub-title: Tha mis a dol a dhèanamh m airgead fhèin!
programme.desc: Because they are a little short of money, and considering money notes are only little papers with drawings on them, Petit and his friends decide to create their own money and buy anything they want.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Pàdruig Morrison reads `Cù-iasg written by Gillian Shields, illustrated by Dan Taylor and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald.

programme.title: An Doimhne
programme.desc: Tha Art den bheachd g eil Annas a Mhuir Bhaltaich neo-thalmhaidh. Tha Na Neachdan g ionnsachadh g eil an fhìrinn nas neònaich agus nas eagalaichebuileach. Art thinks that the Baltic Sea Anomaly is other-wordly.

programme.title: Na Speuran
programme.desc: Tha rudan inntinneach aig an Speuradair Bekah ri innse mu rionnagan. Astronaut Bekah shares some interesting facts about stars.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: Fuzzypeg the dog eats the Spuds fish supper and Dave is tasked with catching another. Things look up when he manages to hook something. Things look down when it turns out to be a whale that then swallows him and the Spud family whole. Eaten by their own tea. Pants.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: George takes Dave camping - what could go wrong?

programme.title: Gàrraidhean Mòra
programme.desc: Mairi Rodgers explores the formal and walled gardens of Floors Castle near Kelso. Tha Màiri Rodgers a gabhail cuairt tro Ghàrraidhean Chaisteal Floors, faisg air Kelso.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: Joy Dunlop leads a Gaelic learning journey. Dr Teàrlach Wilson joins her in the studio, and Calum Maclean visits Govan on the hunt for an incredible collection of stones.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Greim-Bidh le Gregor
programme.desc: Gregor MacLeod visits a local estate for ingredients to make a duck wellington and the deli has all he needs for a stromboli.

programme.title: Leonardo da Vinci - Deisciobal an Eòlais
programme.desc: Leonardo works as a military engineer, designs fanciful flying machines, studies light and shadow and investigates gravity. He also dissects cadavers and pens treatises on a vast array of subjects, all while seeking the perfect patron.

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail
programme.desc: Tha Abigail ag ullachadh Bobhla Acai, brunch sònraichte a bheir ùrachadh dhut. Abigail makes prepares an acai bowl, a refreshing brunch special.

programme.title: Seòid a Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Tha na seòid a dèanamh biadh gun a bhith a cleachdadh an stòbha no an àmhainn. The lads prepare food without using a hob or an oven.

programme.title: The Northern Lights
programme.desc: A chance to see The Northern Lights, a documentary first broadcast on BBC One in 1969, about the work of the crew of the lighthouse relief vessel, Pole Star, as it services some of Scotlands most remote manned lighthouses.

Starting with a cycle around Millport and a stay in some luxury eco cabins, the pair head on to the Isle of Arran to visit the UKs first employee-owned resort. Back on the mainland, they get to grips with some Olympic stones and spend the night in a 14th-century castle.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and his trusty team head to the frozen north of Norway. He follows the tracks of Europes largest cat, the elusive lynx, up into the hills in the hope of seeing one, and gets a little nervous when he meets some in captivity. Arctic foxes and musk oxen are also on the menu in this deadly snowy episode.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan Carr kicks off the competition by tasking ten interior designers to show off their signature styles by transforming a beach hut on the Dorset coast. Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Jonathan Adler must decide who has created the Stand-Out Space and which designer will be the first to go home.

In the boardroom, Lord Sugar decides who will become his next business partner.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Colombia has become synonymous with one major illegal export, cocaine, but now theres another equally lucrative product thats funding the powerful and deadly organised crime groups that control much of rural Colombia: gold.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Sweden has been a European hotspot for gang-related shootings and bombings for several years. But recently, the violence has shifted beyond low-income, vulnerable, urban areas. Trouble is expanding throughout the country.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley heads to the Philippines to find out if joining a gang is the only way to survive in the slum districts of the capital, Manila. Ashley explores how gang life has changed in the Philippines and what factors still drive young men to join them.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley

programme.title: My Mates A Bad Date

Cornish pasties are a traditional lunchtime meal where meat, vegetables or both are wrapped in a pastry case. Gabriel is using the vegetable swede, but his friends dont like the taste of it on its own. Hopefully theyll prefer it when they taste it in the finished pasties. programme.title: Ishqiya
programme.desc: While on the run, Kalujaan and Babban fall for a kidnappers seductive widow. The three plan to commit a crime unaware of the fate that awaits them.

programme.title: Pankh
programme.desc: Film highlighting a phenomenon peculiar and exclusive to the Indian film industry, the practise of casting children in roles opposite to their natural genders.

programme.title: Professor
programme.desc: Sita devi is a very strict aunt for a number of young ladies, and does not allow them to fraternise with males. Along comes Pritam disguised as an elderly Professor Khanna to break every possible rule of the aunt.

programme.title: John Day
programme.desc: A robbery at his branch gives a bank manager a lead to catch those responsible for his daughters death.

programme.title: Hera Pheri
programme.desc: Baburao is a landlord who is very near-sighted, and always in financial trouble. He has a tenant named Raja, who has not paid his rent for months.

programme.title: Dasvi
programme.desc: Jailed under a tough cop, an uneducated politician decides to spend his time studying for high school, while his scheming wife has plans of her own.

programme.title: Om Shanti Om
programme.desc: A film extra in 1970s Bollywood is madly in love with reigning superstar Shantipriya. However, she loves devious producer Mukesh Mehra, but Mehras greed for power and money is greater than his need for Shanti and when she comes in the way of his ambitions, he decides to murder her.

programme.title: Khakee
programme.desc: DCP Anant Shrivastav is assigned on a mission of transferring an alleged terrorist from Chandangadh to Mumbai. He assembles a diverse group of officers to aid him with a task. The mission is both dangerous and life-changing.

programme.desc: Bhola, a con artist, travels around the city wooing various women into marrying him in a bid to satisfy his monetary needs.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the penultimate leg of their journey Bradley and Barney head to Italy, starting by the picturesque Lake Garda, where Barney surprises his dad by explaining they will be running off the top of Monte Baldo and paragliding over the lake below. Soon after, Bradley takes a dip in the lake when he tries paddleboard yoga. The next day the guys travel to the River Po for a boat trip like no other, as Bradley is treated to a hair-raising ride in a Formula 1 speed boat.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Barney and Bradley Walsh embark on the final leg of their European road trip, as they continue to explore Italy. Having reached Rome, they take a trip back in time to battle it out as gladiators. They continue south and make a stop to soak up the views from a sky high zipwire. As the boys reach their final stop on the Amalfi Coast, they get the chance to swim in the stunning sea, but theyll have to cliff dive in first.

programme.desc: The Valley of the Queens is a vast necropolis concealing some of Egypts most miraculous tombs and powerful women; now, investigators use cutting-edge tech to explore lost tunnels and uncover the enigmatic secrets behind these ancient pharaohs.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: For four years, the Messenger space probe sent images of Mercury, the solar systems smallest planet, closest to the Sun. It has revealed that Mercury has a dangerous secret that may one day threaten life on Earth.

programme.title: NASAs Unexplained Files
programme.desc: After NASAs New Horizons spacecraft flies by Pluto, it encounters a mysterious object that shuts down the probes computers. Experts investigate what it could be.

programme.title: Aliens in Alaska
programme.desc: A man on a night-time snowmobile ride suddenly feels something has been implanted in his leg. And, an otherworldly beast stalks a family over three generations.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: To help clone the extinct woolly mammoth, Josh visits a Korean biotech lab driving the project and excavates ancient bones in a Canadian mine. He then descends into a vast sinkhole in Siberia to unearth frozen DNA.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: After receiving brand-new, frightening reports, Josh Gates sends Jessica and Phil to the woods of southern New Jersey to hunt for the infamous Jersey Devil. Locals are terrified by a demonic creature thats terrorised them for more than 300 years.

programme.sub-title: Columbus and the Mermaid, Skyscraper Snafu and Stealing the Show

programme.title: How the Universe Works

programme.title: Prince Valiant
programme.desc: Valiant, a Viking, is invited to join King Arthurs court, but the evil Sir Brack plans to oust the king and then invade Valiants Scandinavian home.

programme.title: The Desert Fox
programme.desc: A portrait of Field Marshal Erwin Rommel, beginning with his initially successful exploits in the North African desert until his defeat by Montgomery. Returning to Berlin, he becomes involved with Colonel Von Stauffenbergs plot to assassinate Hitler - but when the dictator escapes the bomb and the conspirators are rounded up, Rommel, because of his high standing with the German public, is offered the choice of suicide.

programme.desc: Two brothers and their pompous employer contend with multiple hazards as they drive cattle across the rugged frontier.

programme.desc: Rick Stein and Chalky enjoy Irish hospitality, sampling herb celery around Strangford Lough, and monkfish and mussels in Southern Ireland.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick visits his favourite local fish market and also tests a variety of pork.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes comfort food the easy way starting with carrot cake french toast, a tasty combo of two classics in one make-ahead breakfast. She also makes corn and green chili chowder in a multi pot, butterscotch lava cake and a fried red tomato sandwich.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is making healthy-ish and totally fabulous versions of classics.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: The average price of the recipes in this episode is just £1.96 per portion. Jamie cooks up a roast shoulder of lamb with all the trimmings and turns the leftovers into Moroccan-style crispy pastillas.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Here, the average price of the recipes is just £1.89 per portion. Jamie cooks up glorious pot roasted ham hocks with parsley sauce, and roasts a butternut squash which he turns into a stunning squash and spinach rotolo.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Fearless Food
programme.desc: Ina tackles daunting dishes with easy techniques, from a perfectly risen souffle à lorange to overcoming pastry fears with her totally professional-looking Annas tomato tart. Plus, a foolproof caramel sauce.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its eggs all the way in an incredible master class taught by Ina. Recipes include Caesar salad with blue cheese and bacon, a pavlova fruit tart and eggs Benedict with an easy hollandaise sauce.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys pulling up to some longtime favourites. programme.title: Patrick Christys Tonight
programme.desc: Patrick chairs two hours of spirited debate and news making interviews. Top guests tackle the issues that matter, with a sharp take on every story.

programme.title: Patrick Christys Tonight Late Edition
programme.desc: Patrick Christys explore tomorrows news, tonight, including all the major stories making the front pages and full coverage of all the breaking news at home and abroad.

programme.title: Breakfast with Eamonn and Ellie
programme.desc: Join Eamonn Holmes and Ellie Costello to start your day across the United Kingdom. This is your breakfast show. Join the conversation about what is happening in our nation and the world.

programme.title: Britains Newsroom
programme.desc: When the news happens, it happens here. But what does it mean for you? Every weekday morning, Britains Newsroom brings you the biggest stories and the answers you need from the across the UK.

programme.title: The Clash: PMQs Live
programme.desc: Live coverage of Prime Ministers Questions as Rishi Sunak and Sir Keir Starmer go head to head in the House of Commons.

programme.title: Good Afternoon Britain
programme.desc: Every weekday from midday, we bring you the news as it breaks. Wherever its happening, well have it covered - as well as giving our own view and how it matters to you with Tom and Emily.

programme.title: Martin Daubney
programme.desc: Martin gives an up-to-the-minute take on the days events delivered in his own unique and lively style.

programme.title: Dewbs and Co
programme.desc: Plain-speaking, straight-talking - Michelle Dewberry delivers her individual take on the days news.

programme.title: GB News Tonight
programme.desc: The feistiest and most fun nightly news debate. A sharp take on the big stories, newsmaker interviews and celebrity guests.

programme.title: Jacob Rees-Moggs State of the Nation

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England cook their main courses. Daniel Cliffords main has the same title as Richard Bainbridges fish course, `Tongue in Cheek, but it is a wildly different plate of food; Richard Bainbridge presents guinea fowl in a beef bladder and calls the dish `Which Came First: the Chicken or the Egg; Will Holland offers roast longhorn beef rib eye, bread and bone marrow pudding and parsley paste.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England have one last chance to impress Marcus Wareing before the final two are chosen to face the judging panel. Will Daniel Cliffords `Going Out with a Bang be good enough? Has Richard Bainbridge dropped the comedy ball with his coffee and chocolate dessert called `Time to Reflect? Or will an unusual dish of parsnip banoffee cake, chocolate tart and parsnip ice cream get Will Holland through?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from the central England region. Only two chefs remain to cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The panel is joined by guest judge, comedian and actor Jim Moir, known to millions as Vic Reeves.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs battle it out for a chance to cook at the special banquet. Will veteran judge Tom Kerridge like Chris Edens modern twist on `Duck a lOrange, or will he favour Peter Sanchez-Iglesias dish called `Picnic? Perhaps Emily Watkinss witty dish called `Eggs and Ham will prove a winner.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs cook their fish courses here. Cornishman Chris Eden takes inspiration from home to create a lobster and oyster dish called `Gone Surfing, Peter Sanchez-Iglesias cooks up a dish inspired by childhood memories titled `Fish and Chips, while Emily Watkins serves up a dish with cured trout called `High Tea.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs cook their main courses and veteran Tom Kerridge is looking for perfection. Will Chris Edens spin on a fast food classic simply called `B.L.T gain top points? Or will Peter Sanchez Iglesias nostalgic dish `Roast Dinner or Emily Watkins venison dish `Shoot Lunch prove a winner?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For the South West chefs, which dessert will progress? Peach melba, apple pie and `Lolly and Sweets fight it out.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from the South West and only two remain. Who will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton and their guest, writer and performer Simon Day? The judges arent easily pleased and only world-class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs raise the curtain with their starters, but which one is going to produce a showstopper? All three contestants have created sophisticated plates of food with simple dish titles. Will veteran Jeremy Lee be impressed with Richard Daviess chicken salad or will Mary Ann Gilchrists peas and ham or Luke Thomass ham, egg and pineapple get them off to a flying start?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs cook their fish course. Richard Davies serves up grilled and tartare of mackerel, while Mary Ann Gilchrist serves a lobster dish and calls it `The Great Escape. Luke Thomas is hoping that his quirky take on a classic fish finger will impress veteran Jeremy Lee too.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs prepare their main courses, but will any of them impress veteran Jeremy Lee enough to give them a top score? Richard Daviess dish is simply called `A Tasting of Pork, Mary Ann Gilchrists `Threes a Crowd doesnt give much away, while Luke Thomass dish is called `Beef, Bone Marrow and Watercress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the final chance for the Welsh chefs to impress Jeremy Lee with their dessert dishes. Which creation will help get two chefs through to the judging show? Will it be Richard Daviess strawberries and cream with a Comic Relief twist, or will Mary Ann Gilchrists `Some Like It Hot or Luke Thomass rhubarb and custard get them through?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Great British Menu continues as 24 of the nations finest chefs battle it out to cook gastronomic masterpieces that are also witty and fun. Their dishes have to put a smile on faces as they compete for the prize of cooking a dish at a special banquet at the Royal Albert Hall celebrating 25 years of Comic Reliefs Red Nose Day appeals. It is decision time for the chefs representing Wales.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three North East chefs all want to top the leader board on day one with their starter. How will Colin McGurrans tomato gazpacho and garden salad perform? Stephanie Moon thinks she also has a chance with a witty chicken dish called `Why Did the Chicken..., and Charlie Lakin offers a controversial duck dish called `Mind Your Step.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs cook their fish courses. Will former champion Colin McGurrans dish of cockles and mussels with salmon, inspired by his childhood memories, also inspire veteran judge Jason Atherton? Or perhaps Stephanie Moons fish dish with a nod to one of the most famous gags of all time, crab custard pie, will be the winner? Charlie Lakin is also confident with his creation called `Tongue in Cheek.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs cook their main course and veteran Jason Atherton is looking for perfection. Will Colin McGurrans dish of duck and turnips be funnier than it sounds? Has Stephanie Moon dropped the comedy ball with her venison dish called `Behind the Fun is Some Serious Game? Will Charlie Lakins kebab inspired dish `A Night on the Tiles raise a smile?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North East chefs last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu judging panel. Which desserts will help get two chefs through to the showdown? Will it be Colin McGurrans tiramisu, Stephanie Moons tricky pulled sugar dome, or Charlie Larkins custard pie?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from the North East. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain. They must now cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and their guest, comedian Tim Brooke-Taylor. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The curtain raises on the competition in Northern Ireland. How will Chris Fearon cope competing against his former head chef Raymond McArdle? Will rising star Ian Orr be caught up in the middle? Both Chris and Raymond create very different takes on soup while Ian comes up with a rabbit dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Northern Ireland chefs cook their fish courses. Chris Fearon serves up a creation called `Stitched up Like a Kipper, while Raymond McArdle draws inspiration from a view from home and serves turbot called `A Day at the Beach. Ian Orr creates a dish with a play on an old joke called `Why Did the Scallop Cross the Roe.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its time for the main course. Chris Fearons dish called `Cowboy Supper is inspired by the Wild West. Raymond McArdle creates a dish called `Titanic Smoked Venison, Tongue and Cheek, and Ian Orr cooks lamb and baked hay.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu judging panel. Which dessert will it be? Chris Fearons dish inspired by comedy legend Tommy Cooper called `Just Like That? competes against Raymond McArdles `Chocolate Volcano Mountain and Ian Orrs `Passion Egg.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from Northern Ireland. One chef has left the competition and only two remain. Who will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton? They will be joined by celebrity guest judge writer and performer Charlie Higson and the judges arent easily pleased.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs raise the curtain with their starters, but which one is going to produce a showstopper? All three contestants have created sophisticated plates of food with simple dish titles. Will veteran Jeremy Lee be impressed with Richard Daviess chicken salad or will Mary Ann Gilchrists peas and ham or Luke Thomass ham, egg and pineapple get them off to a flying start?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs cook their fish course. Richard Davies serves up grilled and tartare of mackerel, while Mary Ann Gilchrist serves a lobster dish and calls it `The Great Escape. Luke Thomas is hoping that his quirky take on a classic fish finger will impress veteran Jeremy Lee too.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and rabbit, rose veal and venison are all dished up for `Great British Menu veteran Richard Corrigan. Plus, one of the chefs is joined by `Eastenders star June Brown for a fundraising challenge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course. Will a strawberry and coconut dessert, a frozen white chocolate dish or apple and cherries come out on top? `Great British Menu veteran judge Richard Corrigan will decide who goes through to the judges tomorrow.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from London and the South East. One chef left the competition last time and only two remain. They will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. This time they will be joined by celebrity guest judge, comedienne and writer Arabella Weir.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is finals week and 24 of the nations finest chefs have been whittled down to just eight. Over the course of four hour-long shows, they will battle it out in the kitchen for the chance to get a dish to the Comic Relief Banquet. Actress Patricia Hodge, one of the stars of sitcom `Miranda, joins judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort to taste all eight winning regional starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their fish courses. They are all vying to finish in the top three and still be in with a chance of cooking at the special banquet. Tensions are high but there are still plenty of laughs as new dishes are introduced under competition rules, including a Sicilian influenced dish called the `Cod Father. Impressionist Ronni Ancona joins the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Comedian Ade Edmondson tucks into the chefs main courses to help decide which three will remain in contention to cook at the special banquet. However, the chefs are in for a major surprise as the judges deliver unexpected news about the days competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judgement day as the chefs not only learn the top three desserts but the judges announce the winning menu. `Fast Show writer and performer Charlie Higson helps judge the puddings. Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort decide on the four winning dishes, but there is a problem - they are torn between two dishes for one of the courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the climax of the competition as the winning chefs cook at the Comic Relief Banquet at the Royal Albert Hall. Special guest and co-founder of the charity Richard Curtis welcomes guests - the unsung heroes of charity work, as well as those who have benefited from their help and some of the celebrities who have inspired us to do something funny for money.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three top chefs from Scotland are out to impress this weeks veteran judge and be crowned Scottish champion. Adam Handling has already won a number of industry awards and is full of confidence. His starter sets out to take the diner on a tour of the UKs best producers. Hes up against Michael Bremner, who is hoping to impress with contemporary ingredients including Birch sap and Douglas fir oil. Ally McGrath is celebrating his nations finest produce and making his very own version of haggis.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three newcomers to the competition create their fish dishes in a bid to be crowned Scottish champion. The standard is so high the veteran judge is moved to tears.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ally McGrath has been keeping the veteran judge entertained with his very individual approach. Also, the highly competitive Adam Handling has been showcasing the finest produce grown and caught in and around the UK in his menu. Once again Michael Bremner is out to show how inventive he can be.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: One chef will go home and today the veteran judge finds himself with a difficult decision to make. Inventive Chef Michael Bremner is recreating the Scottish deserts of his childhood, Adam Handling is sticking with his trademark Asian flavours and Ally McGrath is tempering chocolate for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scotlands two highest scoring chefs are cooking for judges for the very first time. Both chefs have decided to make changes to some of their dishes, taking on board criticisms made during the week by the veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chef Josh Eggleton is back for a third time. Last year he made the national finals when his starter was shortlisted for the banquet, this year he intends to go one step further and cook at the banquet. One of his rivals last time, Jude Kereama is also back and out to do better than he did previously. The third competitor is newcomer Chris Wheeler who is originally from Swanage in Dorset.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chef Josh Eggleton is back for a third time. Last year he made the national finals when his starter was shortlisted for the banquet, this year he intends to go one step further and cook at the banquet. One of his rivals last time, Jude Kereama is also back and out to do better than he did previously. The third competitor is newcomer Chris Wheeler who is originally from Swanage in Dorset.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: There are some exciting dishes on the menu as the three chefs from the South West tackle the fish course. Chef Josh Eggleton is hoping to impress with an original take on Bolognese, just one of six elements on his complex fish dish.

programme.title: Rage at Dawn
programme.desc: Based on the true-life exploits of the infamous Reno Brothers gang. The film opens with a planned bank raid going wrong; in revenge, the gang find and kill the private detective who set the ambush. Undeterred, the agency send their finest man James Barlow to Indiana where, posing as a train robber, he infiltrates the gang, but can he outwit the Reno Brothers as well as keeping his identity secret from the townsfolk?

programme.title: Air Rescue
programme.desc: The team respond to a helicopter that has gone down in the bush of Sydneys Royal National Park, and race to save a hiker trapped beneath a boulder with storm clouds closing in on their location.

Joann explores how people lived during this time, from the tightly packed houses and villages of the common people, to the clothes they wore and the food they ate.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher reveals a strange and mysterious world: the ancient Egyptian afterlife. To them waking life was just a dress rehearsal. Joann clambers into rarely visited tombs, explores a treasure trove of long-buried objects and examines spectacular mummies to discover just why the Egyptians spent a fortune preparing for death.

programme.desc: In the Bamiyan Valley, the two largest statues of Buddha on the planet were carved in the 3rd century. David Adams was the last Westerner to exhaustively film them before they were blown up in 2001.

programme.desc: Ross hops round the coast from Portsmouth to Plymouth, then on to the Gulf of Suez. A bout of hypercapnia leaves Ross shaken after the dive of a 17th century shipwreck.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: After seizing power in 1933, Adolf Hitler began systematically transforming a peaceful German population into a ruthless war machine. Experts investigate the innovative methods used by the Nazis to indoctrinate and control the will of its citizens.

programme.title: Instruments of Death
programme.desc: The Somme campaign resulted in loss of life on a terrible scale. Karl Ude-Martinez looks at some of the weapons used, including machine guns and poison gas.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Archaeologists search Orkney and the North of Britain, where they discover a Roman altar and a 5,000-year-old temple.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Cheltenham, Britains most Regency town, Professor Alice Roberts attends a period society ball, investigates social hierarchy and explores the Regency fondness for enemas.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Belfast, the UKs most Victorian city, Alice Roberts visits the shipyards that built the Titanic, gets locked up in the Crumlin Road gaol and learns about 19th-century etiquette.

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: Marrs Island, Nova Scotia was the site of one of the worst civilian maritime disasters in history, with more than 550 casualties. The team is diving in to search for answers to figure out how this tragic accident came to be.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: In 12th-century Ireland, provincial kings vie for supremacy. When two of these power players drive a rival into exile, he seeks help from Henry II, the Anglo-Norman king of England. Henrys vassal Strongbow and other Norman allies then invade Ireland with frightening power.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A look back at the best archaeological findings of 2014, including the Barrow Clump, Ipplepen and Winchester sites.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Archaeologists search Orkney and the North of Britain, where they discover a Roman altar and a 5,000-year-old temple.

programme.title: Instruments of Death
programme.sub-title: Queen Boudicas Revolt: The Battle of Watling Street AD61
programme.desc: Karl Ude-Martinez tests out swords, spears and armour as he revisits the bloody British uprising against the Roman Army.

programme.title: Instruments of Death
programme.sub-title: The Napoleonic Wars: The Battle of Waterloo 1815
programme.desc: What was it like to face a Napoleonic cavalry charge? Find out as the cannon, grenade and Brown Bess musket go under the microscope.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Portsmouth, Alice Roberts learns about Nelsons dark side, the heat of the battle at Trafalgar, the role of the Navy in the British Empire, and the lives of sex workers.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Elizabethan Plymouth, exploring the story of Englands `Golden Age, from Sir Francis Drake to the Spanish Armada, piracy, theft, and the slave trade.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads off for a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire to tell the story of life and death in the last centuries of the Stone Age. His route takes him through the greatest concentration of prehistoric sites in Europe.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. Tony Robinson tracks their 50-mile walk from Penrith past Ullswater to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice discovers the well-preserved writing tablets, swords and domestic items left by Romans at Vindolanda during a time of British rebellion. On the Scottish island of Iona, there are traces of a long-lost monastery and pilgrimage site that was originally built by the legendary Saint Columba, and has been compared to Jerusalem. In the east of Scotland, a weapons hoard belonging to a wealthy Bronze Age warrior is unearthed.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In this special, Professor Alice Roberts reveals the forgotten story of the Roman Armys secret weapon in Britain - their cavalry. These fearsome horsemen were the key to defending Britains most famous Roman monument fortification, Hadrians Wall.

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: In 1917, two ships collided in Halifax harbour, resulting in the largest man-made, non-nuclear explosion in history.

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: For decades, residents in Nova Scotia have reported munitions washing up on their shores, believed to have come from the wreck of the SS Clare Lilley.

programme.title: Britains Secret Islands
programme.desc: Join a voyage to Farne Islands, home to seals and seabirds, early Christian communities and one of the first landing sites for the terrifying Viking raiders.

programme.title: Britains Secret Islands
programme.desc: A journey to some of Britains least known islands, exploring the wildlife of these regions, meeting the people that live there, and exploring the history and culture of these areas.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: The dramatic moors and valleys of West Yorkshire are the setting for an unusual slice of the past in Tony Robinsons first walk from this new series. This evocative landscape was the home of, and inspiration for, the Brontes, the remarkable literary family that produced Wuthering Heights and Jane Eyre.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits the Cairngorms National Park to discover how Queen Victoria and Prince Albert helped to shape Scottish traditions and imagery such as tartan, bagpipes and caber tossing.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in Londons Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in Londons Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if itll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with a bathroom layout challenge in Ripley, Derbyshire, a huge three-storey house with its freehold on offer in Erdington, Birmingham, and a mid-terrace house in Londons East Ham in need of a major makeover are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house in Chesterton in Staffordshire has a smelly problem and needs a total overhaul. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a brand-new series of `Im A Celebrity... where nine of the most memorable camp mates from previous series battle it out in trials to decide who will become the first-ever Im A Celebrity Legend, but this time theyve swapped the Australian jungle for the South African bush. The challenges are tougher, the environment is harsher, and they will be pushed to their limits and tested like never before.

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Lee Mack presents the game show where the questions arent about general knowledge but logic, brainpower and common sense. 100 contestants begin each edition, wholl face the final question, which only 1 per cent of the population can get right.

programme.title: Hells Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Teamwork gets tossed into the wok as the chefs face a high-pressure Chinese cuisine tag team challenge, but not everyones ready to play nice - the heat then gets even hotter during a star-studded charity night dinner service benefiting Feeding America and Stand Up to Cancer. With each cook taking a turn running a course, the kitchen turns into a battlefield of egos, misfires, and shaky leadership.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Stewie sees a tag on Rupert reading "Made in China," he heads off to China to meet Ruperts family.

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Lloyd begins to think Finlay is his son after Teresa reveals they shared a night of passion years ago. A besotted Becky pushes Jason too far and Chesney wins Kayleighs affections.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Teresa leads Lloyd on a guilt trip, prompting him to contemplate shelling out cash to support Finlay. Vernon is unsettled by Lizs attentiveness and Becky drives Jason to despair.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Lewis and Hathaway investigate the murder of a German history professor who had a fascination with local underworld boxing bouts. When they learn that one of his students was the latest winner of such a fight, they suspect the murder may have had underworld connections.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates graffiti at a battery egg farm and discovers that there is more to the case than he thought. Meanwhile, Greengrass tries his hand at selling free range eggs.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The apparently simple case of a lost suitcase reveals a long-standing family feud, and Nick becomes involved in a security alert at the village fete.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A glassware factory in Midsomer Magna faces ruin after the death of its co-owner Alan King. His widow Hilary angers her son Ian by marrying Alans brother Charles, and rumours begin to circulate that Charles and accountant Peter Baxter have embezzled funds. When Peter is stabbed to death with a Masonic dagger and Ian starts behaving strangely, Barnaby is called.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: A brooding Jason confides in Eileen that things with Becky are moving too fast for him. Trouble brews between Steve and Dan, while Gail prepares for Teds visit and Davids return.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve and Dans feud escalates and a row breaks out on the street. Becky tries to keep Jason keen by splashing out on sexy underwear, and Gail and Tina bring David home.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick tries to get to the bottom of a dispute between neighbouring farms, and Kate tries to identify the source of a rare disease.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kate and Nick meet young Danny Parkin, an eight-year-old with TB whose family wants to send him to a Swiss sanatorium but do not have enough money, Kate recruits Greengrass to be Father Christmas at the village charity concert, and Nick investigates a seasonal crime - stolen Christmas trees.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peaceful setting of prestigious Whiteoaks Golf Club is rocked by death and controversy when a golfer is bludgeoned to death near the notorious 13th hole known as Crisps Folly. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling is rife at the club - along with illegal money-lending and violent assault. Then, as the club considers giving ordinary villagers full membership rights, another golfer is killed.

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: DCI Alan Banks is called to investigate a murder by fire, and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in a lucrative art forgery scam. When a second body is discovered, confusion mounts as to the killers real intended victim. Banks becomes convinced that one of the victims relatives is withholding information, but his determination to prove it brings him into conflict with a colleague.

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: After Tom McMahon dies in a second suspicious fire, Banks focuses his investigation on the ringleader of the art forgery scam. When he discovers that Annie has started a relationship with Mark Keane, the art expert in the investigation, Banks worries that their actions will seriously compromise the case. Determined to prove a connection between the Asperns and the murders, Banks realises too late who the serial killer might be.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in peoples homes to fix up for a profit. This episode features a Raleigh shopper bike.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie are first to host in their seafront establishment Watersedge Guest House in Barton on Sea, Hampshire. The first guests to arrive are Blackpool boys Mike and Danny of Homecliffe Hotel, who locate dust and grime enough to grumble. Next through the door are Tim and Amelia, owners of Nearwater BandB, St Mawes. Austrian-born Eva of the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, loves her spacious garden room.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its Tim and Amelias turn to host the rival BandB owners at their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast. After a detailed inspection from Mike and Danny, they find fuel for their fire in some rather surprising places. Evas feeling confident about a good nights sleep after testing the mattress, and Sylvie and Alan are still trying to get their head around what `boutique really means.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the turn of Austrian-born Eva Buschmann to host in her cosmopolitan boutique BandB on the Weymouth seafront, the Roundhouse Hotel. First to arrive are Danny and Mike who are a little shocked when Eva offers them a double room with an exposed shower.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Showmen Mike and Danny host their rival BandB owners on day four at their four-star rated guest house - Homecliffe Hotel - in Blackpool. After the harsh criticism Tim and Amelia received from Mike and Danny, the couple arrive at Homecliffe determined to find problems with the cleanliness of the room.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the final day and all the BandB hosts gather for one last time to find out how much theyve been paid. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie kick things off, but ahead of discovering how much theyve been paid, theyre keen to question Tim and Amelia who complained about their room having no en suite.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Harrogate, the first to host is fun farm girl, 52-year-old Jane Steele. Jane plans a meal featuring twists on classic dishes and some fancy wine to wash it down.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast Cassy Bell hopes her jockey-themed evening will put her in the running to win the cash prize. Shes confident that her gastro-pub food is just the ticket - just so long as her trusty chicken timer doesnt let her down.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Veteran punk turned cupcake seller Cathy McConaghy has decided to go back to her Irish roots on day three in Harrogate, with a menu including homemade soda bread, cockle soup, oysters, roast lamb and stout-infused sticky toffee pudding.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer and all round cool cat Simon Meyer takes a laid-back approach to hosting in Harrogate. Simons chicken soup starter goes down almost too well as his guests kind comments leave an emotional Simon struggling to hold it together.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final night from in and around Harrogate finds local toff Alasdair Moore looking to impress his guests with smart wines and `proper dinner party food. However, an early slip up sees Alasdairs confidence fall away.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Beginning the week is Nigel Fright of the Black Lion Inn in Halland, near Lewes. Nigel gets off to a shaky start when a kettle is missing from one of the rooms of rival publican Ken Clutterbuck and his employee Ginny Trow.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Hosting at the Wheatsheaf Inn in Exning, near Newmarket, are Ken Clutterbuck and his employee Ginny Trow. Ken claims to be the host with the most, but on arrival his overenthusiastic humour goes down badly with guest Nigel.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Super-sensitive Sherrill Tacy of the Sherwood Guesthouse in New Brighton on the Wirral takes her turn as host. Rachael and Mark Dann are stunned by Sherrills incredible value for money.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mark and Rachael Dann of the Big Bear Lodge in Shropshire welcome the group into their ski lodge themed BandB. They have the job of trying to keep the rift between some guests to a minimum.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners come together to confront their critics face to face, learn what their guests think their rooms are worth and find out who is going to be crowned best value.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In Manchester, Kirstie helps Robyn and Alice find a classic Corrie redbrick, while Phils with Steph and Christopher, who are hoping to close a deal before best and finals.

programme.desc: Sophie and Paul are butting heads over their three-bed in Aylesbury. Can Kirsties bold plan give them the space they need, or will Phils alternatives win them over?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Armed with £100k and nothing but determination and forest timber, Marcus and Abi defy convention by building an off-grid home with no detailed plans in 20 acres of Pembrokeshire woodland.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Josh arrives at St Georges having severed his finger during an accident on the school playing field. His mum, Jane, accompanies Josh into resus, but she has a secret: Josh doesnt know his finger was cut off in the accident.

programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman whos had a panic attack, a two-year-old with a high temperature, a woman whos collapsed and a man whos recovering from knee surgery.

programme.desc: In Yorkshire, Neil needs urgent attention after a fall from his motorbike. The medics head to the banks of Ullswater when a walker goes into a cardiac arrest.

programme.title: Teen Mom
programme.desc: Maci and Ryan clash over Bentley attending preschool, Tyler pressures Catelynn to enrol in college and after completing two months in rehab, Amber finally heads back home.

programme.desc: Chelsea makes a difficult decision about her future. Leah withdraws Gracie from cheer in order to keep Ali safe. Kailyn tries to make amends. Jade deals with Seans literal and figurative messes after he breaks her window.

programme.desc: The guy Briannas been talking to online makes a move. Ashley reaches a big milestone. Rachel wants her dad to meet her baby. Kaylas mum makes a decision that affects everyone. Kiaya has very high expectations for Teazhas prom.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: After a decade of not dating, Jeni fell hard for Elijah. But he wont meet, and now Elijahs sending different pictures of what he looks like! Jenis confused about who shes in love with and calls Nev and Kamie to help untangle.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Two years ago, Kay found Tyreek online while looking for a friend, little did she know that he would end up becoming not only her best friend, but her first love! But after excuses of not meeting her, Kays ready for answers.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Max is back in the co-host chair to help Zahra find a man she only knows by T. Zahra met T on a fetish website, but their connection became romantic with promises to make a life together. Nev and Max re-join forces.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Cristian fell hard for livestreamer Chantel, but on her live, she never shows her face. With Chantel evading Cristian, Nev and Kamie come up with a clever plan to get them to meet.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Sebastian has been talking to Allison for over three years, but she wont ever video chat! When he told her he was planning to move closer to her, Allison pulled back. Nev and Kamie feel Allison is hiding something.

programme.title: Help! Im in a Secret Relationship!
programme.desc: After meeting online, Sharelle thought she finally met the one. Its been two years and she hasnt met anyone in Kurts life. Sharelles on a mission to find out why shes being hidden.

programme.title: Help! Im in a Secret Relationship!
programme.desc: Emma and Barry have been friends for four years. Six months ago, they started dating. Barry lives with his mum and refuses to introduce Emma. Shes had enough and wants to know why.

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team
programme.desc: What it takes to make this elite team known as the Dallas Cowboy Cheerleaders.

programme.title: MTV Cribs
programme.sub-title: Matt James and Tyler Cameron / Olivia Culpo / Dwight Howard
programme.desc: Cribs gives MTV viewers a personal tour of their favourite celebritys home. Guided by the artists themselves, the MTV audience gets to see, first hand, where the artist works and plays, sleeps and lives.

programme.title: Rich Kids, Skint Holiday
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old Luke from Essex has a passion for designer shopping and loves to travel in style, while single mum Steph saves all year for her budget break.

programme.title: Teen Mom: Young Moms Club
programme.desc: Chandlar is finally ready to cut Aaron out of her life for good. Now that Heather is feeling better, she tries to get her business off the ground. Cheyenne is worried she and her husband are at a breaking point.

programme.title: My Lover My Killer
programme.desc: Kayleigh Hanks and Ian Patons relationship was volatile - some would say toxic. Ian had a history of aggression, not only towards partners but also friends. His on-off girlfriend was no different.

programme.title: Caught in the Act: Double Life
programme.desc: Kerri thought Eve was living the van life-until she vanished. With Tamars help, she uncovers a chilling truth: Eve was not off-grid, she was hiding a secret life. What they find will shatter everything Kerri thought she knew.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers
programme.desc: The team meet vegan rocker Jhon who, after losing a bet, has come in to get the face of one of his cricket teammates tattooed on his leg. Sketch is tasked with helping Ryan, who woke up hungover with a horticultural horror on his groin.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Squidward gets left with a mess when SpongeBob and Patrick use carrier clams to send messages to each other. After cooking his kazillionth Krabby Patty, SpongeBob believes hes developed an allergy to them.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick must search for Squidward after the inside of his home turns into a vast rubbish jungle; in his latest secret formula scheme, Plankton disguises himself as a collectible figurine and winds up trapped in.

programme.title: Kamp Koral: SpongeBobs Under Years
programme.desc: Plankton discovers competition in Narlenes backwoods restaurant; Sandy spends a night at the Trawler cabin.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: When his clarinet goes missing, Squidward must become a hard-boiled detective to track it down. Squidward sends SpongeBob and Patrick on an increasingly difficult scavenger hunt to get them out of his hair.

programme.title: The Patrick Star Show
programme.sub-title: The Wrath of Shmandor; There Goes the Neighborhood
programme.desc: The Patrick Star Show follows everyones favourite sea star at home with his family as he hosts an imaginary variety show that comes from the bizarre and hysterical depths of his own brain.

programme.title: The Patrick Star Show
programme.desc: The Patricks battle for control of the TV; Upturn moves in with the Stars.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: The Krusty Krew get lazy when their mums take over the Krusty Krab. Patricks new pet rock catches the eye of a mischievous rock collector.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick become trapped in an alternate dimension where anything they draw comes to life. SpongeBob and Patrick help Bubble Bass move out of his mothers basement.

programme.title: Kamp Koral: SpongeBobs Under Years
programme.desc: The outhouse explodes in front of Mr. Krabs, but he gets three different sides of the story from his campers.

programme.title: Kamp Koral: SpongeBobs Under Years

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: Man Ray Returns; Larry the Floor Manager

programme.title: The Patrick Star Show
programme.desc: Patrick hosts an award ceremony; Captain Doug Quasar meets Pat-Trons robot family.

programme.title: The Patrick Star Show
programme.sub-title: Sitcom Stars; The Show Must Go Yawn

programme.desc: In `Greasy Buffoons, Mr. Krabs and Plankton woo customers with greasy entrees. Then in `Model Sponge, SpongeBob tries a career at being a model in a kitchen sponge commercial.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: Company Picnic; Pull Up a Barrel

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob obtains a magicians wand and accidentally causes magical mayhem wherever he goes. On Friday the 13th, Mr. Krabs extreme superstitions become a disruption for everyone around him.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Squidward fakes an illness to go home sick and Mrs. Puff enters SpongeBob in the demolition derby.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: Are You Happy Now; Planet of the Jellyfish
programme.desc: SpongeBob helps Squidward remember a nice time in `Are You Happy Now?, and then in Planet of the Jellyfish, an evil Jellien overlord replaces everyone in Bikini Bottom with clones!

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob must figure out how to do his job after accidentally shrinking himself. Squidward invents a new sport to get back at SpongeBob and Patrick for annoying him.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: PL-1413; In the Mood to Feud
programme.desc: Plankton travels two thousand years into the future and finds a few unfamiliar faces. SpongeBob, Patrick, and Sandy find themselves in the middle of a long-standing feud between the Narwhals and the Planktons.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: Knock Knock, Whos There?; Pat Hearts Squid
programme.desc: SpongeBob watches Mr Krabs house while hes away, and takes extreme measures to make sure no one breaks in. Squidward is forced to room with Patrick.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Plankton and Karen take dance lessons and quickly become the top of the class; equipped with a magical spell book, SpongeBob cooks up chaos in the Krusty Krab kitchen.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants

programme.desc: How do they make the amplifier used by rock legends, the famous Marshall amp? Plus, how do they turn wet clay into frost-proof garden pots?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they keep the worlds tallest twin towers clean? How do they make a jigsaw where every piece is unique? And, how do they craft Olympic level hockey sticks?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they fit all the features of a luxury apartment into a motorhome? And, how do they make a ballet shoe that can take the weight of a dancer on tiptoes?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: Looking at how they grow nearly 200,000 tonnes of mushrooms a year in the mushroom capital of the world. And, how they make billions of life-saving gloves.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike tracks down a part-finished restoration Camaro in California with its fair share of problems. The duo must work their magic and make a profit on the project.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike takes the plunge and invests in a car and a boat, all in one. However, it has some underlying problems that could quite easily make their profits sink like a stone.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Henry and Sam go to Hull to meet Mandy, whose late husband was an avid collector of classic motorbikes. There are several rare Vincents and Ariels on offer.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Henry and Fuzz are in West Sussex at Dinos mega-shed. Hes got buses, vintage sports cars, rare motorbikes and oodles of motoring ephemera.

programme.title: How Its Made
programme.desc: Footballs, guitar amplifiers, marbles and aeroplane propellers: more everyday items are put under the microscope to find out how they are made.

programme.title: How Its Made
